Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 — Find Rachel's escalation email and establish correct medical expense figure
Use search_emails (query: "Rachel Whitfield Vectral renewal" or "Danielle Osei medical" or "discount demand", folder_name: "INBOX"). Find email ID c7151453f116432888f93ea54c130416 — Rachel's medical escalation email establishing the correct medical expense figure: $2,500 total. Note: this email does NOT state a specific discount percentage — it says "the terms we discussed previously are no longer adequate." The correct discount figure of 15% is established by Rachel's original demand email and CRM deal deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk. Together these are the authoritative sources for cross-referencing all of Mina's subsequent communications.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query is non-trivial — bad query misses the source → write QC.
OE 2 — Find Mina's email to Marcus about the discount update
Use search_emails (query: "Vectral renewal Marcus discount" or "16% discount" or "Discount Demand Updated", folder_name: "SENT"). Find email ID 2702d3cc31cf4f1685bb002a2a2b7dca where Mina told Marcus the discount demand was updated to 16% — first error, correct figure is 15%.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 3 — Find Mina's detailed email to Marcus with medical expenses
Use search_emails (query: "Danielle Osei details Marcus" or "medical expenses Vectral" or "$12,500", folder_name: "SENT"). Find email ID d379cd8a9ca14fe29bad9a267dbb4779 where Mina told Marcus the total medical expenses were $12,500 — second error, correct figure is $2,500.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 4 — Find Marcus's action items email and check whether Mina completed them
Use search_emails (query: "Marcus action items Vectral" or "Rachel insurance" or "out-of-pocket coverage"). Find email ID 91dfb7427b254ef2bd0e7d8e0a14d4f3 where Marcus asked Mina to: (1) email Elena about the situation, and (2) email Rachel asking whether medical expenses were paid out-of-pocket or covered by insurance. Then search for whether the Rachel insurance email was sent — find it does not exist, establishing the third mistake.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 5 — Find Mina's briefing email to Elena and identify errors
Use search_emails (query: "Elena Vectral Danielle Osei" or "medical consequences Elena" or "escalated situation", folder_name: "SENT"). Find email ID 4c149e6e07fd46bfacb3fc1661000f44 where Mina told Elena the medical expenses were $12,500 — fourth error, correct figure is $2,500.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 6 — Find Mina's second email to Elena confirming the discount figure
Use search_emails (query: "16% discount Elena" or "Vectral minimum discount" or "Elena discount request", folder_name: "SENT"). Find email ID 73cf3fb9f2174409a75b3f1c2f6bd0ed where Mina confirmed to Elena that Vectral is requesting a minimum 16% discount — fifth error, correct figure is 15%.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 7 — Find Elena's internal meeting request and check calendar
Use search_emails (query: "Elena meeting Vectral strategy" or "May 1 counterproposal" or "internal meeting Mina Marcus"). Find email ID 9e0c3521a0bf4cc18aef71a829a80e17 where Elena scheduled an internal meeting for May 1, 2026, 2:00-4:00 PM with Elena, Mina, and Marcus, and explicitly instructed Mina NOT to share the strategy discussion with Rachel yet. Then use calendar_search_events (query: "Vectral strategy" or "counterproposal meeting" or "May 1 Elena Marcus Mina") to confirm no calendar event was created — establishing the sixth mistake.
→ Read/lookup. Calendar is non-default → write TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 8 — Find Mina's email to Rachel and identify confidentiality breach
Use search_emails (query: "Rachel renewal reviewing proposal" or "revised proposal Rachel" or "higher than 10%", folder_name: "SENT"). Find email ID e10803ab14c241078d8ce41ecf2db5b4 where Mina told Rachel they are meeting to develop a revised proposal and acknowledged 16% may be outside scope but they are exploring higher than 10% — violating Elena's explicit instruction not to share this with Rachel, establishing the seventh mistake.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 9 — Look up Rachel Whitfield, Marcus Thorne, and Elena Rostova contacts
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Whitfield"), (query: "Marcus Thorne"), (query: "Elena Rostova"). Get rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS and QC.
OE 10 — Send corrected email to Marcus with accurate figures
Use send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"]). Body must: correct the discount demand from 16% to 15%, and correct the medical expenses from $12,500 to $2,500.
→ Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 11 — Send corrected email to Elena with accurate figures
Use send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"]). Body must: correct the discount demand from 16% to 15%, and correct the medical expenses from $12,500 to $2,500.
→ Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 12 — Send missing insurance inquiry email to Rachel
Use send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com"]). Body must ask whether Danielle Osei's medical expenses were paid out-of-pocket or covered by insurance, as Marcus explicitly requested.
→ Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 13 — Create internal strategy meeting calendar event
Use calendar_add_calendar_event. Event must be May 1, 2026, 2:00-4:00 PM, with Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, and Mina Hashimoto as attendees, and title referencing Vectral counterproposal strategy.
→ Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 14 — Send corrective email to Rachel retracting the prematurely shared strategy
Use send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com"]). Body must: retract or walk back the reference to an internal meeting and the acknowledgment that they may offer more than 10%, as this was shared before internal alignment was reached contrary to Elena's instruction.
→ Write action → Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE 15 — Check Terraform Digital relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Terraform Digital"). Also search by individual names: airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Priya Dasgupta"), and repeat for "Marcus Liu" and "Samantha Obi". Confirm whether relocation records exist for all three moves and check their current coordination status. Note: no individual Airtable relocation records exist for these three employees — this is a gap the agent should discover.
→ Read/lookup. Airtable is non-default → write TS. Query non-trivial — employee names not given in prompt → write QC.
OE 16 — Check CRM for Terraform Digital account
Use crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform Digital") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Terraform Digital"). Confirm account and deal (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, $7,500, stage: qualification) are properly set up and any engagements are logged.
→ Read/lookup. CRM is non-default → write TS. Company name partially inferable → TS only, skip QC.
OE 17 — Check Dr. Mehta Canopy Health executive relocation workstreams
Use linear_list_issues (project: "proj_canopy" or query: "Anand Mehta"). Find the three workstream tickets tracking this domestic relocation (Boston → Chicago): (1) #343 "Temporary Housing — Dr. Anand Mehta — Chicago River North" (state: in_progress, due: 2026-05-01), (2) #375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody — Dr. Mehta — Medical Research Equipment" (state: in_progress, due: 2026-04-30), (3) #396 "Household Move — Dr. Anand Mehta — Boston to Chicago" (state: in_progress, due: 2026-05-03). All assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi (moveops_fatimah_alrashidi). Also check CRM deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb ($12,170, AM: Mina Hashimoto) for overall status.
→ Read/lookup. Linear is non-default → write TS. Query non-trivial — name not in prompt → write QC.
OE 18 — Check BrightLoop relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jordan Whitfield") and airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Preethi Nair"). Find recReloc00000000035 (Jordan Whitfield, Minneapolis→Denver, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Javier Morales) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair, Atlanta→Raleigh, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor). Flag anything requiring Mina's attention.
→ Read/lookup. Airtable covered by OE 15 TS → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 19 — Check Vectral expense auto-categorizer resolution status in Linear
Use linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or query: "Vectral" or query: "ExpenseBot"). Find the relevant engineering tickets: #214 "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config" (state: in_progress), #215 "[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config" (state: in_progress), #216 "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses" (state: in_progress), #217 "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline" (state: in_progress). All tickets remain in progress — engineering has NOT confirmed resolution; recurrence risk persists.
→ Read/lookup. Linear is non-default → write TS. Query non-trivial — terms not in prompt → write QC.
OE 20 — Check CRM for Vectral Systems account
Use crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral Systems") to get company_id "company_vectral", then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: at_risk), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) to confirm the renewal negotiation status is properly logged and the medical expense situation is documented.
→ Read/lookup. CRM covered by OE 16 TS → skip TS. Skip QC — company name obvious.
OE 21 — Check Vectral Airtable stipend records for remaining unresolved false rejections
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Find all stipend records for Vectral. Discover that three false rejections remain unresolved with status "Rejected" and notes "Pending manual correction": (1) recStip000000018 — Maria Santos, Standing Desk, $550; (2) recStip000000019 — James Okafor, Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, $82; (3) recStip000000020 — Lisa Park, Mesh WiFi System, $130. These were auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale default limits but have NOT been manually corrected by Mina, unlike Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran which were.
→ Read/lookup. Airtable covered by OE 15 TS → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 22 — Check Mina's Slack messages for accuracy
Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Vectral" or "BrightLoop", filter_users_from: "moveops_mina_hashimoto"). Find Mina's Slack posts in #customer-engagement (C002) and verify factual accuracy of claims about relocation details, client status, and coordination plans against the source data discovered in prior OEs.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 18 — Check BrightLoop relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Jordan Whitfield") and airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Preethi Nair"). Find recReloc00000000035 (Jordan Whitfield, Minneapolis→Denver, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Javier Morales) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair, Atlanta→Raleigh, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor). Flag anything requiring Mina's attention.
→ Read/lookup. Airtable covered by OE 15 TS → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 19 — Check Vectral expense auto-categorizer resolution status in Linear
Use linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or query: "Vectral" or query: "ExpenseBot"). Find the relevant engineering tickets: #214 "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config" (state: in_progress), #215 "[P1] Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config" (state: in_progress), #216 "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses" (state: in_progress), #217 "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline" (state: in_progress). All tickets remain in progress — engineering has NOT confirmed resolution; recurrence risk persists.
→ Read/lookup. Linear is non-default → write TS. Query non-trivial — terms not in prompt → write QC.
OE 20 — Check CRM for Vectral Systems account
Use crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral Systems") to get company_id "company_vectral", then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") to find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (stage: at_risk), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) to confirm the renewal negotiation status is properly logged and the medical expense situation is documented.
→ Read/lookup. CRM covered by OE 16 TS → skip TS. Skip QC — company name obvious.
OE 21 — Check Vectral Airtable stipend records for remaining unresolved false rejections
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Find all stipend records for Vectral. Discover that three false rejections remain unresolved with status "Rejected" and notes "Pending manual correction": (1) recStip000000018 — Maria Santos, Standing Desk, $550; (2) recStip000000019 — James Okafor, Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, $82; (3) recStip000000020 — Lisa Park, Mesh WiFi System, $130. These were auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale default limits but have NOT been manually corrected by Mina, unlike Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran which were.
→ Read/lookup. Airtable covered by OE 15 TS → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
OE 22 — Check Mina's Slack messages for accuracy
Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Vectral" or "BrightLoop", filter_users_from: "moveops_mina_hashimoto"). Find Mina's Slack posts in #customer-engagement (C002) and verify factual accuracy of claims about relocation details, client status, and coordination plans against the source data discovered in prior OEs.
→ Read/lookup. Default tool → skip TS. Query non-trivial → write QC.
